original: Anthony sees a rhythm of work, prayer, work, prayer.
english: Despair is answered by a rhythm the body can keep.
spanish: La desesperación se responde con un ritmo que el cuerpo puede sostener.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 1
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, pp. 1-2. Short working rendering; copyright-gated, avoid long translated excerpts.

original: Anthony is told not to inquire into hidden judgments, but to attend to himself.
english: The mind is saved first by returning from cosmic questions to its own watch.
spanish: La mente se salva primero al volver de las preguntas cósmicas a su propia vigilancia.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 2
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 2. Short working rendering; copyright-gated, avoid long translated excerpts.

original: Anthony gives three precepts: remember God, follow Scripture, remain in place.
english: Keep God before the eyes, let action be tested, and do not flee too quickly.
spanish: Ten a Dios ante los ojos, prueba la acción, y no huyas demasiado pronto.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 3
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 2. Short working rendering; copyright-gated, avoid long translated excerpts.

original: Anthony says the great work is to take blame for one's sins and expect temptation to the end.
english: The work lasts until the last breath: own the fault and expect the trial.
spanish: La obra dura hasta el último aliento: asumir la falta y esperar la prueba.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 4
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 2. Short working rendering; copyright-gated, avoid long translated excerpts.

original: Anthony says no one enters the kingdom without temptation.
english: Without pressure, the gate of transformation never opens.
spanish: Sin presión, la puerta de la transformación nunca se abre.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 5
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 2. Short working rendering; copyright-gated, avoid long translated excerpts.

original: Anthony tells Pambo not to trust self-righteousness, but to govern tongue and stomach.
english: Do not build holiness on self-trust; begin with appetite and speech.
spanish: No construyas santidad sobre la autoconfianza; empieza por el apetito y la palabra.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 6
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 2. Short working rendering; copyright-gated, avoid long translated excerpts.

original: Anthony sees snares spread across the world and hears that humility passes through them.
english: Where every path is netted, humility becomes the only opening.
spanish: Donde todos los caminos están tendidos como redes, la humildad se vuelve la única abertura.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 7
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 2. Short working rendering; copyright-gated, avoid long translated excerpts.

original: Anthony warns that bodily affliction without discernment can remain far from God.
english: Severity without discernment only hardens the distance.
spanish: La severidad sin discernimiento sólo endurece la distancia.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 8
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 2. Short working rendering; copyright-gated, avoid long translated excerpts.

original: Anthony says life and death are with the neighbor.
english: To wound the brother is to wound the place where God was waiting.
spanish: Herir al hermano es herir el lugar donde Dios esperaba.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 9
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 2. Short working rendering; copyright-gated, avoid long translated excerpts.

original: Anthony compares monks outside the cell to fish too long out of water.
english: Return to the cell before the inner sea dries around you.
spanish: Vuelve a la celda antes de que el mar interior se seque a tu alrededor.
recommendedEditionSource: Benedicta Ward — The Sayings of the Desert Fathers, Anthony 10
notes: Mode A queue from digitized Ward PDF, p. 2. Short working rendering; copyright-gated, avoid long translated excerpts.
